This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants. intro: Rhynchodia slootenii, also known as New Zealand buttonbush, is a plant species within the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and coughs, due to its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Rhynchodia slootenii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues and other health conditions.
